tool_name: WPS Preheat Temperature & PWHT Requirements — Carbon Equivalent & Hydrogen Risk

inputs.name: Carbon Content (C)
inputs.note: From mill certificate; structural steel: 0.10–0.25%; HSLA: 0.05–0.18%

inputs.name: Welding Heat Input (HI)
inputs.note: HI = U × I × η / (1000 × v); SMAW η=0.80; GMAW η=0.80; SAW η=0.99

inputs.name: Diffusible Hydrogen Level (H_d)
inputs.uncertainty_source: ISO 2768-1:1989 — Nominal/material selection, no measurement uncertainty

formulas: CE_IIW = C_pct + Mn_pct/6 + (Si_pct + Cr_pct)/24 + Mo_Ni_pct/40   // [dimensionless] Carbon Equivalent — IIW Formula (CE_IIW) Reference: IIW / AWS D1.1-2020 Commentary — CE = C + Mn/6 + (Si+Cr)/24 + Mo/4 + Ni/40 + Cu/13

CET = C_pct + (Mn_pct + Mo_Ni_pct * 0.5 + Cr_pct * 0.1) / 10   // [dimensionless] Carbon Equivalent — CET (EN 1011-2 Annex C) Reference: EN 1011-2:2001 Annex C — CET used for preheat calculation by CET method

T_p_AWS_c = (CE_IIW < 0.40) ? 10 : (CE_IIW < 0.45) ? 66 : (CE_IIW < 0.60) ? 107 : 150   // [°C] Preheat Temperature — AWS D1.1 CE Method (T_p_AWS) Reference: AWS D1.1-2020 Table 3.2 — minimum preheat by CE and thickness category

T_p_EN_c = 697 * CET + 160 * Math.tanh(plate_thickness_mm / 35) + 62 * Math.pow(H_content, 0.35) - 328   // [°C] Preheat Temperature — EN 1011-2 CET Method (T_p_EN) Reference: EN 1011-2:2001 Annex C Eq.(C.3) — T_p = 697×CET + 160×tanh(t/35) + 62×H^0.35 - 328

T_p_governing_c = Math.max(T_p_AWS_c, T_p_EN_c, 10)   // [°C] Governing Minimum Preheat Temperature (T_p_governing) Reference: Conservative: maximum of AWS D1.1 and EN 1011-2 requirements; minimum 10°C per standards

PWHT_required = (CE_IIW > 0.45 && plate_thickness_mm > 32) || (plate_thickness_mm > 38 && property === 'Class D/E') ? 'PWHT REQUIRED per ASME IX / AWS D1.1 §5.8' : 'PWHT not mandatory — verify per design code'   // [text] PWHT Requirement Flag Reference: ASME B31.3-2022 §331.1.1 — P-No.1 PWHT required above 19 mm (¾ in) for pressure piping

V1.error_msg: CE_IIW > 0.65: very high hardenability — cold cracking risk is severe; mandatory H2.5 consumables, preheat ≥ 150°C, and post-heat treatment required

V2.error_msg: Preheat temperature > 250°C: verify interpass temperature control ≤ 300°C — excessive interpass causes heat-affected zone toughness loss per EN 1011-2

V3.error_msg: Very low heat input on hardenable steel: rapid cooling rate — HAZ hardness may exceed 350 HV; increase HI or raise preheat to slow cooling

smart_warnings.message: CE > 0.50 + H_d > 10 ml/100g: hydrogen-assisted cold cracking risk — switch to H5 or H2.5 consumables immediately; minimum 48h inspection delay before NDT
High preheat + high heat input: creep in HAZ possible during multipass welding — implement interpass temperature monitoring ≤ T_p + 100°C
Heavy section with low calculated preheat: heat sink effect underestimated — add 25°C for plates > 50 mm per AWS D1.1 Commentary §C-3.5
CET > 0.45: HSLA or alloy steel with elevated cold cracking susceptibility — document in WPS and PQR; hydrogen bake-out at 200°C × 2h strongly recommended
Standard hydrogen consumable on alloy steel: upgrade to H10 minimum — AWS D1.1 Table 4.5 restricts H15 for CE > 0.40 without mandatory elevated preheat

fmea.effect: CE_IIW > 0.65: very high hardenability — cold cracking risk is severe; mandatory H2.5 consumables, preheat ≥ 150°C, and post-heat treatment required
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Preheat temperature > 250°C: verify interpass temperature control ≤ 300°C — excessive interpass causes heat-affected zone toughness loss per EN 1011-2
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.effect: Very low heat input on hardenable steel: rapid cooling rate — HAZ hardness may exceed 350 HV; increase HI or raise preheat to slow cooling
fmea.control_measure: Input validation — automatic BLOCK on violation

fmea.failureMode: CE > 0.50 + H_d > 10 ml/100g: hydrogen-assisted cold cracking risk — switch to H5 or H2.5 consumables immediately; minimum 48h inspection delay before NDT
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: CE_IIW > 0.50 && H_content > 10

fmea.failureMode: High preheat + high heat input: creep in HAZ possible during multipass welding — implement interpass temperature monitoring ≤ T_p + 100°C
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: T_p_governing_c > 150 && HI_kj_mm > 3.0

fmea.failureMode: Heavy section with low calculated preheat: heat sink effect underestimated — add 25°C for plates > 50 mm per AWS D1.1 Commentary §C-3.5
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: plate_thickness_mm > 50 && T_p_governing_c < 100

fmea.failureMode: CET > 0.45: HSLA or alloy steel with elevated cold cracking susceptibility — document in WPS and PQR; hydrogen bake-out at 200°C × 2h strongly recommended
Standard hydrogen consumable on alloy steel: upgrade to H10 minimum — AWS D1.1 Table 4.5 restricts H15 for CE > 0.40 without mandatory elevated preheat
fmea.effect: Smart warning triggered when: H_content === 15 && CE_IIW > 0.40
